skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides an AI assistant through collaborative product and technical brainstorming before implementation. It asks focused questions, compares approaches, validates design sections, and records the approved design.
skill.summary: Plan features and technical designs through structured dialogue before implementation.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives caused by ordinary prose in SKILL.md, especially repeated use of the word design. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The skill does instruct the agent to inspect project context, write a design document, and commit it to git, so it carries medium risk through filesystem and external command behavior.

medium_findings.title: Repository Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to write a validated design to docs/plans and commit the design document to git. This is a legitimate planning workflow, but it can modify the workspace and create commits if the user grants tool access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs document creation and a git commit. The behavior is not malicious, but it is a real filesystem and external command capability.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur in normal planning prose, such as descriptions of design and documentation. No cryptographic code, weak algorithm selection, or security-sensitive implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only Markdown guidance and no cryptographic API calls or algorithm names used for implementation. The repeated matches align with prose terms rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The detected Ruby or shell backtick pattern is a Markdown code span around a destination file path, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. The line describes where to write a design document.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 38 is inside a Markdown bullet and contains a backticked path. There is no script file, interpreter context, or command substitution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Commit Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to commit the generated design document to git. This external command usage should require normal user oversight before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The git commit instruction is explicit and actionable. It is a standard development task, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Unclear ideas often turn into weak requirements and rework. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code shape ideas into validated designs before implementation starts.
actual_capabilities: Reviews current project context before proposing a design.
Asks one focused question at a time to refine requirements.
Compares two or three approaches with trade-offs and recommendations.
Presents design sections in short validated increments.
Covers architecture, components, data flow, errors, and testing.
Can write the approved design to a dated document path.
limitations: It does not implement the feature by itself.
It depends on user answers for product goals and constraints.
It may read project files when tool access is available.
It may suggest a git commit after writing the design document.

use_cases.title: Shape a Product Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a rough feature idea into requirements, options, and a validated design.

use_cases.title: Plan a Code Change
use_cases.description: Explore architecture, data flow, error handling, and tests before editing code.

use_cases.title: Prepare Team Review
use_cases.description: Create a concise design document that can be reviewed before implementation starts.

prompt_templates.title: Explore a New Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me explore this idea: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to compare two or three approaches for [goal]. Include trade-offs and a recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Technical Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to create a technical design for [feature]. Cover architecture, data flow, errors, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Document a Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to validate this plan in sections, then document the approved design: [plan summary].

output_examples.input: I want to add saved filters to our dashboard.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks who uses the filters, what must be saved, and whether sharing is required. It then compares local storage, user profile storage, and a shared database model.

output_examples.input: Plan a migration from polling to webhooks.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines event flow, retry behavior, signature validation, failure modes, rollout steps, and tests. It validates each section before moving on.

output_examples.input: Create a design document for a redesigned onboarding flow.
output_examples.output: The assistant turns approved decisions into a dated design document with goals, components, data needs, edge cases, and implementation notes.

best_practices: Answer each question with concrete constraints and success criteria.
Review each design section before asking the assistant to continue.
Confirm before allowing file writes or git commits.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when requirements are already final and implementation should begin.
Do not skip the question phase for complex or ambiguous features.
Do not let it commit changes without reviewing the generated document.

faq.question: Does this skill write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on requirements, options, and design before implementation.

faq.question: Can it inspect my repository?
faq.answer: Yes, if the assistant has tool access. It asks to review files, docs, and recent commits.

faq.question: Does it make git commits?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to commit the design document after validation. Review changes before approving that step.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-engineering work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure product, workflow, and documentation ideas, although the examples are technical.

faq.question: How detailed are the designs?
faq.answer: The skill asks for sections of 200 to 300 words and covers architecture, components, data flow, errors, and testing.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to reduce confusion and help users answer clearly during planning.
